what: Recipe 1: Embed ggpaintr with the default binders
title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: closest supported equivalent ggpaintr_server(), lets place controls outputs inside page layout.
code: library(ggpaintr) library(shiny)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Embedded ggpaintr"),   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       ggpaintr_controls_ui()     ),     mainPanel(       ggpaintr_outputs_ui()     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   paintr_state <- ggpaintr_server_state(     "ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +       geom_point() +       labs(title = text)"   )    ggpaintr_bind_control_panel(input, output, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_draw(input, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_export(output, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_plot(output, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_error(output, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_code(output, paintr_state) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

code: ids <- ggpaintr_ids(   control_panel = "builder_controls",   draw_button = "render_plot",   export_button = "export_app",   plot_output = "main_plot",   error_output = "main_error",   code_output = "main_code" )  ids ui <- fluidPage(   sidebarLayout(     sidebarPanel(       ggpaintr_controls_ui(ids = ids)     ),     mainPanel(       ggpaintr_outputs_ui(ids = ids)     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   paintr_state <- ggpaintr_server_state(     "ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) + geom_point()",     ids = ids   )    ggpaintr_bind_control_panel(input, output, paintr_state, ids = ids)   ggpaintr_bind_draw(input, paintr_state, ids = ids)   ggpaintr_bind_export(output, paintr_state, ids = ids)   ggpaintr_bind_plot(output, paintr_state, ids = ids)   ggpaintr_bind_error(output, paintr_state, ids = ids)   ggpaintr_bind_code(output, paintr_state, ids = ids) }

what: Recipe 3: Customize the returned plot in renderPlot()
title: ggpaintr Extensibility
text: default ggpaintr_bind_plot() binder preserves current wrapper behavior. want modify built plot rendering, write renderPlot() use ggpaintr_plot_value(). ggpaintr_plot_value() returns raw ggplot object success NULL otherwise. keeps advanced customization seam side-effect free lets decide render failure states app.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   paintr_state <- ggpaintr_server_state(     "ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) + geom_point()"   )    ggpaintr_bind_control_panel(input, output, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_draw(input, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_export(output, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_error(output, paintr_state)   ggpaintr_bind_code(output, paintr_state)    output$outputPlot <- renderPlot({     plot_obj <- ggpaintr_plot_value(paintr_state$runtime())      if (is.null(plot_obj)) {       plot.new()       return(invisible(NULL))     }      plot_obj + ggplot2::theme_minimal()   }) }

title: ggpaintr Placeholder Registry
text: ggpaintr now supports per-app placeholder registries. means downstream developers can define new placeholder , pass paintr_formula(), ggpaintr_app(), ggpaintr_server_state(), generate_shiny(), keep rest package runtime unchanged. public entry points layer : ggpaintr_placeholder() ggpaintr_effective_placeholders() ggpaintr_missing_expr() Built-placeholders var, text, num, expr, upload now use registry path custom placeholders.

title: ggpaintr Placeholder Registry
text: date placeholder adds Shiny dateInput() control turns chosen date .Date("YYYY-MM-DD") generated code.
code: sales <- data.frame(   day = as.Date("2024-01-01") + 0:4,   value = c(10, 13, 12, 16, 18) )  date_placeholder <- ggpaintr_placeholder(   keyword = "date",   build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {     shiny::dateInput(id, copy$label)   },   resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(as.character(value), "")) {       return(ggpaintr_missing_expr())     }      rlang::expr(as.Date(!!as.character(value)))   },   copy_defaults = list(label = "Choose a date for {param}") )  placeholders <- ggpaintr_effective_placeholders(   list(date = date_placeholder) )  names(placeholders) #> [1] "var"    "text"   "num"    "expr"   "upload" "date"

what: Using the registry in parsing and runtime
title: ggpaintr Placeholder Registry
text: Pass registry placeholders = ... anywhere want custom placeholder support. registry can also used higher-level wrappers.
code: obj <- paintr_formula(   "ggplot(data = sales, aes(x = day, y = value)) +     geom_line() +     geom_vline(xintercept = date)",   placeholders = placeholders )  runtime <- paintr_build_runtime(   obj,   list(     "geom_line+checkbox" = TRUE,     "geom_vline+checkbox" = TRUE,     "geom_vline+2" = as.Date("2024-01-03")   ) )  runtime$code_text #> [1] "ggplot(data = sales, aes(x = day, y = value)) +\n  geom_line() +\n  geom_vline(xintercept = as.Date(\"2024-01-03\"))" ggpaintr_app(   "ggplot(data = sales, aes(x = day, y = value)) +     geom_line() +     geom_vline(xintercept = date)",   placeholders = placeholders )

title: ggpaintr Placeholder Registry
text: Every placeholder occurrence becomes one metadata record paintr_obj$placeholder_map. metadata record contains: id: Shiny input id placeholder occurrence keyword: placeholder keyword, date layer_name: layer placeholder found param: argument name, xintercept index_path: expression path used replacement fields passed every placeholder hook custom placeholder code can behave differently parameter layer.

title: ggpaintr Placeholder Registry
text: Use resolve_input() raw Shiny input needs preprocessing handed resolve_expr(). omitted, ggpaintr uses input[[id]].
code: trimmed_text_placeholder <- ggpaintr_placeholder(   keyword = "trimmed_text",   build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {     shiny::textInput(id, copy$label)   },   resolve_input = function(input, id, meta, context) {     trimws(input[[id]])   },   resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {     if (identical(value, "")) {       return(ggpaintr_missing_expr())     }      rlang::expr(!!value)   } )

title: ggpaintr Placeholder Registry
text: Use bind_ui() deferred dynamic UI registration. built-var placeholder registers delayed renderUI() outputs data known.
code: deferred_placeholder <- ggpaintr_placeholder(   keyword = "dynamic_text",   build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {     shiny::uiOutput(paste0("dynamic-", id))   },   bind_ui = function(input, output, metas, context) {     for (meta in metas) {       output[[paste0("dynamic-", meta$id)]] <- shiny::renderUI({         shiny::textInput(meta$id, paste("Dynamic control for", meta$param))       })     }      invisible(NULL)   },   resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {       return(ggpaintr_missing_expr())     }      rlang::expr(!!value)   } )

title: ggpaintr Placeholder Registry
text: Use prepare_eval_env() placeholder needs inject objects evaluation environment plot built. built-upload placeholder assigns uploaded data objects code evaluated.
code: prepared_data_placeholder <- ggpaintr_placeholder(   keyword = "prepared_data",   build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {     shiny::textInput(id, copy$label, placeholder = "dataset_name")   },   resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {     if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {       return(ggpaintr_missing_expr())     }      rlang::parse_expr(value)   },   prepare_eval_env = function(input, metas, eval_env, context) {     eval_env$prepared_dataset <- data.frame(x = 1:3, y = c(2, 4, 3))     eval_env   } )

code: date_copy_rules <- paintr_effective_copy_rules(   list(     defaults = list(date = list(label = "Pick any date")),     params = list(       xintercept = list(date = list(label = "Reference date"))     ),     layers = list(       geom_vline = list(         date = list(           xintercept = list(help = "Choose a cutoff date for the vertical guide.")         )       )     )   ),   placeholders = placeholders )  paintr_resolve_copy(   "control",   keyword = "date",   layer_name = "geom_vline",   param = "xintercept",   copy_rules = date_copy_rules,   placeholders = placeholders ) #> $label #> [1] "Reference date" #>  #> $help #> [1] "Choose a cutoff date for the vertical guide."
